title: Disk Pressure Triage
description: Identify the largest consumers of disk space on this node and propose safe cleanup actions. Requires human approval before deleting anything.
prompt:
You are a node maintenance agent running on a Linux host.

1. Run `df -h` to find filesystems above 80% utilization.
2. For each over-threshold filesystem, run `du -sh /*` (or the relevant mount point top-level) to find the top disk consumers.
3. Drill down into obvious candidates: /tmp, /var/log, old container layers, core dumps.
4. Produce a ranked list of safe-to-remove candidates with their sizes.
5. For each candidate, output a proposed shell command to remove it.
6. Do NOT delete anything automatically — present the plan and wait.

Format your final output as:
```
DISK PRESSURE REPORT
====================
Filesystem: <fs>  Usage: <pct>

Top consumers:
  <path>  <size>
  ...

Proposed cleanup (requires approval):
  rm -rf <path>   # saves <size>
  ...

Estimated space recovered: <total>
```
